stem: Equilateral triangle ABC has a side length of 12. Point D is the midpoint of side BC, and point E lies on segment AD such that angle ABE = 30 degrees. What is the length of BE?
explanation: Since D is the midpoint of BC in equilateral triangle ABC, AD is the altitude. This gives BD = 6 and AD = 6 * sqrt(3). In right triangle ABD (right angle at D), angle ABD = 60 degrees. Since angle ABE = 30 degrees and E lies on AD, the angle DBE = angle ABD - angle ABE = 60 - 30 = 30 degrees. In right triangle BDE (right angle at D), tan(30 degrees) = DE / BD, so DE = 6 * tan(30 degrees) = 6 * (1 / sqrt(3)) = 6 * sqrt(3) / 3 = 2 * sqrt(3). Then BE = BD / cos(30 degrees) = 6 / (sqrt(3)/2) = 12 / sqrt(3) = 12 * sqrt(3) / 3 = 4 * sqrt(3). Verification: BE^2 = BD^2 + DE^2 = 36 + 12 = 48, so BE = sqrt(48) = 4 * sqrt(3).

distractorRationale.A: Correct: Decomposes angle ABD = 60 degrees into angle DBE = 30 degrees, then uses right triangle BDE to find BE = BD / cos(30) = 6 / (sqrt(3)/2) = 4 * sqrt(3) = 4\sqrt{3}
distractorRationale.B: Correctly finds DE = 2 * sqrt(3) using tan(30) = DE/BD in right triangle BDE, but reports DE as the answer instead of computing BE = sqrt(BD^2 + DE^2) = 4 * sqrt(3), yielding the incorrect answer = 2\sqrt{3}
distractorRationale.C: Uses the full altitude AD = 6 * sqrt(3) as the answer, confusing the altitude of the equilateral triangle with the segment BE = 6\sqrt{3}
distractorRationale.D: Uses sin(30) = BD / BE, yielding BE = BD / sin(30) = 6 / (1/2) = 12, but this misidentifies BD as opposite the 30-degree angle when BD is actually adjacent = 12

imageDescription: Equilateral triangle ABC with vertices labeled. Point D is marked as the midpoint of BC with a right angle symbol at D showing AD is perpendicular to BC. Point E is on segment AD between A and D. Segment BE is drawn. Angle ABE is labeled 30 degrees. Side length AB is labeled 12.
